# NLM context used
Act 1 Shattered Keep is a cold, structured fortress damaged by ritual catastrophe: cool granite floor, real perimeter walls, real arch gates, moss/debris near edges, and a clear combat center.
Warblade canon is a dark-haired, broad-shouldered male melee class with a massive two-handed greatsword, low-guard stance, heavy readable silhouette, and 30-35 deg ARPG camera.
Elementalist canon is an unarmed female hand-caster with cool indigo/deep teal clothing, controlled casting posture, honey-blonde bun, and runtime cyan/violet/gold VFX rather than a staff.
Imp canon is a small swarm enemy with a large head, pointed limbs, dark body, cyan rift leakage, and a lunge-first first-encounter combat read.

# Visual inspection
Floor flat + plain + no visible grid? YES
Characters readable at chunky pixel scale? YES
35 deg camera angle consistent across floor/walls/characters? YES
Cool muted palette respected? YES
L4 overlay placement semantic (gates + walls + clear center)? YES
Hades-feel atmospheric? YES

# Iteration count
1 built-in image_gen call

# Critique (honest)
The result is passable as an in-game reference: it has strong room readability, a dark flat combat floor, two real arch gates, clear center action, readable Warblade/Elementalist/Imp silhouettes, and strong Hades-like atmosphere. Biggest gap from target is that the walls and arch stones are more detailed and brick-like than the strict no-grid production floor/wall language, and the Warblade armor skews black steel more than the canonical dark leather/brass identity. The concept still works for mood, composition, value isolation, gate placement, and combat staging.

# Next-step recommendation
Use this as an art bible reference for Act 1 combat-room mood, gate scale, clear-center layout, and VFX value contrast. For production asset prompts, tighten the wall language toward irregular painterly stone masses, reduce individual block repetition, and push Warblade closer to dark leather with brass accents.
